name: Untitled
description: From Hugging Face Blog
curator_note: Experimental Hugging Face tool via RSS import, ideal for testing latest model updates.

name: Quoting SpaceX S-1
description: We have the ability to use compute resources to support our proprietary AI applications (such as Grok 5, which is currently being trained at COLOSSUS II), while also providing access to select compute capacity to third-party customers. For example, in May 2026, we entered in
curator_note: Reveals SpaceX's AI infrastructure strategy, a unique peek into industry practice.

name: How fast is 10 tokens per second really?
description:
How fast is 10 tokens per second really?
Neat little HTML app by Mike Veerman (source code here) which simulates LLM token output speeds from 5/second to 800/second.
Useful if you see a model advertised as "30 tokens/second" and want to get a feel for what that actually loo
curator_note: Intuitive speed benchmark for LLM token output rates
_raw_title: How fast is 10 tokens per second really?

name: Google I/O, Gemini Spark, Antigravity
description:
It's hard to find much to write about Google I/O this year because I have a policy of not writing about anything that I can't try out myself, and a lot of the big announcements are "coming soon".
I actually prefer to write about things that are in general availability, becau
curator_note: Lack of concrete demos limits real-world applicability for now

name: llm-gemini 0.32
description:
Release: llm-gemini 0.32
        

New model gemini-3.5-flash for Gemini 3.5 Flash.


See also my notes on Gemini 3.5 Flash, and the pelican I drew using this upgrade to the plugin.
    
    
        Tags: gemini, llm
curator_note: Gemini 3.5 Flash model integration unlocks rapid prototyping for conversational interfaces.

name: Gemini 3.5 Flash: more expensive, but Google plan to use it for everything
description:
Today at Google I/O, Google released Gemini 3.5 Flash. This one skipped the -preview modifier and went straight to general availability, and Google appear to be using it for a whole lot of their key products:

3.5 Flash is available today to billions of people globally:

For
curator_note: Google's general-purpose LLM choice, signaling industry shift.
_raw_title: Gemini 3.5 Flash: more expensive, but Google plan to use it for everything

name: datasette-llm-accountant 0.1a4
description:
Release: datasette-llm-accountant 0.1a4
        

Fixed bug tracking chains of responses. Refs datasette-llm#7


    
    
        Tags: llm, datasette
curator_note: Tracks response chains, key for conversational LLM analysis and debugging.

name: llm-gemini 0.32a0
description:
Release: llm-gemini 0.32a0
        

Compatible with llm>=0.32a0 alpha - adds the ability to stream reasoning tokens.


    
    
        Tags: gemini, llm
curator_note: Streams reasoning tokens, a key enabler for real-time inference pipelines.

name: datasette-llm 0.1a8
description:
Release: datasette-llm 0.1a8
        

Fix for bug where llm_prompt_context() hook did not fully collect chains of responses. #7
curator_note: Fixes response chain collection bug, enhances LLM context handling.

name: The last six months in LLMs in five minutes
description:
I put together these annotated slides from my five minute lightning talk at PyCon US 2026, using the latest iteration of my annotated presentation tool.


  
  #
  I presented this lightning talk at PyCon US 2026, attempting to summarize the last six months of developments i
curator_note: Rapid LLM advancements distilled into 5-minute summary, ideal for quickly grasping recent developments.
_raw_title: The last six months in LLMs in five minutes

name: Glaucous-winged Gull, Brown Pelican, Snowy Egret, Canada Goose
description: Glaucous-winged Gull, Brown Pelican, Snowy Egret, Canada Goose, in Los Angeles River, CA, USI'm heading home from PyCon US today so I went on a last morning walk to try and spot a pelican. I saw one! Didn't get a great photo of that, but I did see some goslings down
curator_note: Rare bird species dataset for environmental AI applications.
_raw_title: Glaucous-winged Gull, Brown Pelican, Snowy Egret, Canada Goose

name: GDS weighs in on the NHS's decision to retreat from Open Source
description:
GDS weighs in on the NHS's decision to retreat from Open Source
Terence Eden continues his coverage of the NHS' poorly considered decision to close down access to their open source repositories in response to vulnerabilities reported to them as part of Project Glasswin
curator_note: cautionary tale of open source abandonment in healthcare IT
_raw_title: GDS weighs in on the NHS's decision to retreat from Open Source

name: Warelay -> OpenClaw
description:
In preparation for a lightning talk I'm giving at PyCon US this afternoon I decided to figure out how many names OpenClaw has actually had since that first commit back in November.
Thanks to this first_line_history.py tool (code here) the answer, according to the Git history
curator_note: Tracks project name changes via Git history, ideal for monitoring legacy code evolution.

name: Quoting Julia Evans
description:
[...] in the last 10 years I’ve learned to really love and respect CSS as a technology.
So I decided years ago that I wanted to react to “CSS is hard” by getting better at CSS and taking it seriously as a technology, instead of devaluing it. Doing that changed everything for
curator_note: Rare dev mindset shift: embracing underappreciated tech like CSS.

name: inaturalist-clumper 0.1
description:
Release: inaturalist-clumper 0.1
        Part of the infrastructure I use for publishing my iNaturalist sightings on my blog. I've been running this in production for a few weeks now, inspiring some iterations on how it works, so I decided to ship a 0.1 release.
You
curator_note: Simplifies iNaturalist RSS import for blog publishing workflows.

name: Western Gull, Rock Pigeon
description: Western Gull, Rock Pigeon, in Los Angeles Area (custom), CA, USI went for a bird walk in the morning before PyCon, and we spotted a local seagull enjoying a Starbucks.
curator_note: Bird species mapping via crowdsourced morning walks, novel data collection approach

name: QR code generator
description:
Tool: QR code generator
        Claude helped me build this tool for creating QR codes, for both text/URLs and for connecting to WiFi networks.

    
    
        Tags: vibe-coding, tools, generative-ai, ai, llms
curator_note: Simplifies WiFi network sharing via QR codes, a underrated use case.

name: datasette-llm-limits 0.1a0
description:
Release: datasette-llm-limits 0.1a0
        This plugin works in conjunction with datasette-llm and datasette-llm-accountant to let you configure a per-user (or global) spending limit for LLM usage inside of Datasette. Configuration looks something like this:
plugin
curator_note: Enforces per-user LLM usage limits, crucial for cost control in shared Datasette deployments.

name: Not so locked in any more
description:
This Mitchell Hashimoto quote about Bun migrating from Zig to Rust reminded me of a similar conversation I had at a conference last week.
I was talking to someone who worked for a medium sized technology company with a pair of legacy/legendary iPhone and Android apps.
They t
curator_note: Illuminates Rust's growing role in replacing niche languages like Zig.
_raw_title: Not so locked in any more

name: Quoting Mitchell Hashimoto
description: [...] On the interesting side is how fungible programming languages are nowadays. Programming languages used to be LOCK IN, and they're increasingly not so. You think the Bun rewrite in Rust is good for Rust? Bun has shown they can be in probably any language they want in ro
curator_note: Language lock-in fading fast, toolkit agility gains.

name: datasette-ip-rate-limit 0.1a0
description:
Release: datasette-ip-rate-limit 0.1a0
        The datasette.io site was being hammered by poorly-behaved crawlers, so I had Codex (GPT-5.5 xhigh) build a configurable rate limiting plugin to block IPs that were hammering specific areas of the site too quickly.
Here
curator_note: Blocks abusive crawlers with Codex-built rate limiting, ideal for high-traffic datasets.
